evidence: During the fiscal years ended June 30, 2025, 2024 and 2023, we invested approximately $1.388 billion, $1.276 billion, and $1.195 billion, respectively, in research and development... including investments in generative AI
we continued deploying AI tools in our products and across our sales, service, and research and development functions to improve the client experience and drive internal productivity gains
We have made and expect to continue to make significant investments in product development and new technologies
ADP Assist validates payroll information, checking for payroll anomalies and using generative AI to identify and help resolve missing tax registrations
we extended AI tools to all of our product developers for coding, testing, and documentation

ADP DataCloud analyzes aggregated, anonymized and timely HCM and compensation data from more than one million organizations across the U.S., powering solutions that provide clients with in-depth workforce and business insights
if the data used to train a model or the model s output is inaccurate or biased, or alleged to be inaccurate or biased, we could be subject to reputational damage or litigation
Our use of generative AI in our products and operations also introduces additional risks, including risks related to accuracy, bias, transparency, security, and privacy
use of AI tools may result in the release of confidential or proprietary information which could limit our ability to protect... our intellectual property rights

capex_mentions.quote: invested approximately $1.388 billion, $1.276 billion, and $1.195 billion, respectively, in research and development... including investments in generative AI
capex_mentions.context: R&D spend for fiscal years 2025, 2024, 2023 respectively, explicitly including generative AI investments

limitations: R&D figures are total R&D, not AI-only; AI-attributable share is not separately quantified, reducing precision of investment intensity score

ai_narrative_concreteness scored 4 rather than 5 because no quantified business outcomes (%, $, time saved) are provided for deployed AI use cases — awards and product descriptions are present but metrics are absent

Risk disclosure includes named risk categories and specific mechanisms (bias/litigation, IP leakage, regulatory compliance costs) justifying score 4; no quantified risk exposure or named oversight body with formal mandate beyond the AI Data Ethics Committee advisory role
